mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at very low levels, the discount rate on equities stays compressed and risk appetite improves, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully priced and equity returns do not improve after the easing move.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in CPIAUCSL indicates inflation pressure that pushes policy expectations tighter and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should disappear when inflation is stable or falling.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker income growth, and higher recession probability, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when unemployment is climbing rather than merely elevated.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of episodes where UNRATE has risen by 0.3pp or more over 3 months, or the conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST signals broad domestic demand weakness across retail spending and housing activity, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if only one of the two series weakens or if equities are already in a post-shock rebound.
failure_condition: The combined RSAFS-HOUST weakness signal does not outperform each single series alone, or triggered 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO after a prior slump indicates supply-side recovery and higher expected earnings capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal should be absent if industrial production is flat or declining.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises on a 3-month moving-average basis after a prior decline
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of episodes where INDPRO turns up after a 3-month decline, or the conditional average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and PAYEMS both weaken together, housing demand and labor income are simultaneously softening, which should precede weaker equity returns over the next month. The interaction matters because the labor effect is most informative when housing activity also rolls over.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-PAYEMS weakness state does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than either series alone, or the joint conditional mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary regime, a high FEDFUNDS level above neutral pressure reduces equity valuation and should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be strongest when rates are already elevated and liquidity is tightening.
failure_condition: Short-horizon SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the 5-day conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, low unemployment combined with continuing payroll gains supports positive investor sentiment and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if payroll growth weakens while UNRATE stays low.
failure_condition: The combined low-UNRATE/high-PAYEMS state does not yield positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or the interaction adds no explanatory power versus PAYEMS alone.
